title: [Feature]: Add WebSocket connection configuration guardrails
problem: nats-sinks validates ws:// and wss:// schemes, and nats-py supports WebSocket transport, but the project does not yet provide certified WebSocket-specific configuration guardrails. Operators need a clear fail-closed model before using WebSocket transport in production.
proposal: Add explicit WebSocket transport guardrails for URL scheme selection, mixed WebSocket and non-WebSocket seed URL rejection before nats-py connection attempts, wss:// TLS context construction with local CA support, production warnings for ws://, and redacted effective-configuration output. Keep transport selection separate from sink and ACK behavior.
users: Operators in constrained network environments, security reviewers, and maintainers certifying NATS client transport modes.
delivery_semantics: Transport validation must not change commit-then-acknowledge behavior. WebSocket transport must still deliver raw messages to the same runner loop, normalize envelopes, write sinks durably, and ACK only after success.
security: Prefer wss:// for production, validate local CA paths, reject mixed transport lists before connecting, redact all connection details, and document proxy and TLS limitations. Do not allow credentials in URLs.
acceptance: ws:// and wss:// behavior is explicitly documented as configured transport rather than incidental URL validation.
Mixed WebSocket and non-WebSocket seed URLs fail configuration validation before connection setup.
wss:// with tls_ca_file builds a verified TLS context.
ws:// is documented as local-lab or explicitly accepted controlled-network use only.
tests: Add unit tests for ws://, wss://, mixed transport lists, local CA context construction, redacted output, and unchanged nats-py option construction for normal nats:// and tls:// deployments. Run scripts/check.sh.
documentation: Update docs/websocket-connection-evaluation.md, docs/nats-connections.md, docs/configuration.md, docs/security.md, docs/operations.md, docs/nats-feature-gap-analysis.md, ROADMAP.md, and CHANGELOG.md.
closeout: Close after the release containing certified WebSocket configuration guardrails is published. Include tests, documentation, and known limitations.
